structuredFacts.title: Interstellar (film)
structuredFacts.subtitle: 2014 film by Christopher Nolan
structuredFacts.leadParagraph:
Interstellar is a 2014 epic science fiction film directed by Christopher Nolan, who co-wrote the screenplay with his brother Jonathan Nolan. It has an ensemble cast led by Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway, Jessica Chastain, Bill Irwin, Ellen Burstyn, and Michael Caine. Set in a dystopian future where Earth is suffering from catastrophic blight and famine, the film follows a group of astronauts who travel through space in search of a new home for humanity.
The film began development in 2006 from a treatment by producer Lynda Obst and theoretical physicist Kip Thorne, and was originally set to be directed by Steven Spielberg. Thorne served as an executive producer and scientific consultant on the film, and wrote the tie-in book The Science of Interstellar. It was Obst's final film as producer before her death. Cinematographer Hoyte van Hoytema shot it on 35 mm film in the Panavision anamorphic format and IMAX 70 mm. Filming began in late 2013 and took place in Alberta, Klaustur, and Los Angeles. Interstellar uses extensive practical and miniature effects, and the company DNEG created additional visual effects.
Interstellar premiered at the TCL Chinese Theatre on October 26, 2014, and was released in theaters in the United States on November 5, and in the United Kingdom on November 7, with Paramount Pictures distributing in the United States and Warner Bros. Pictures distributing in international markets. In the United States, it was first released on film stock, expanding to venues using digital projectors. It was a commercial success, grossing $681 million worldwide during its initial theatrical run, and $774.7 million worldwide with subsequent releases, making it the 10th-highest-grossing film of 2014. The film received generally positive reviews from critics. Among its various accolades, Interstellar was nominated for five awards at the 87th Academy Awards, winning Best Visual Effects.
structuredFacts.briefSummary: Although Steven Spielberg was originally attached to direct a new science fiction film in 2006—a project born from a treatment by producer Lynda Obst and theoretical physicist Kip Thorne—Christopher Nolan ultimately took the helm for the 2014 epic, Interstellar. Starring Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway, and Jessica Chastain, the film is set in the dystopian year of 2067, where Earth ravages under catastrophic blight, dust storms, and severe famine. When former pilot Cooper is recruited to guide a spacecraft through a wormhole near Saturn, a desperate journey begins. Ultimately, the film grossed over $774 million worldwide and won the Academy Award for Best Visual Effects.

cards.title: Interstellar and the Cosmic Odyssey
cards.summary: Directed by Christopher Nolan, the 2014 epic science fiction film Interstellar stars Matthew McConaughey and Anne Hathaway. Humanity faces extinction on a dying Earth in the dystopian future of 2067, threatened by a catastrophic blight and famine. This grim setting serves as the backdrop for a world plagued by severe, relentless starvation. To save the human race, NASA dispatches a crew of astronauts through a wormhole near Saturn in search of a habitable planet. Former pilot Cooper is recruited for this secret mission, tasked with steering a spacecraft through the distant cosmic gateway. Ultimately, the survival of the species depends on this desperate journey beyond our galaxy.
cards.readerQuestion: What is the narrative premise of the film and who are the key characters embarking on this cosmic journey?
cards.keyTakeaway: Humanity's survival depends on a desperate journey beyond our galaxy.

cards.title: From Blind Date To IMAX
cards.summary: In 1980, Carl Sagan arranged a blind date between producer Lynda Obst and theoretical physicist Kip Thorne. This unexpected pairing sparked a scientifically grounded film concept that soon attracted top-tier Hollywood directors. Although Steven Spielberg was originally attached to direct when development first started in 2006, the project eventually evolved far beyond this early phase. It ultimately became a visually spectacular reality captured through advanced large-format film techniques. Cinematographer Hoyte van Hoytema shot the movie using a mix of Panavision anamorphic and IMAX 70 mm. In the end, scientific collaboration and bold cinematography defined the film's physical creation.
cards.readerQuestion: How did a scientific partnership and cinematic vision shape the physical creation of the film?
cards.keyTakeaway: Scientific collaboration and bold cinematography defined the film's physical creation.

cards.title: Interstellar's Global Launch
cards.summary: The TCL Chinese Theatre hosted the film's world premiere on October 26, 2014 . Paramount Pictures and Warner Bros. Pictures partnered to launch Christopher Nolan's sci-fi epic globally . The theatrical rollout began in the United States on November 5, 2014, and reached the United Kingdom on November 7, 2014 . This strategic release helped the film gross hundreds of millions of dollars during its initial run . Paramount Pictures and Warner Bros. Pictures served as the major studio distribution partners for the theatrical release . Ultimately, Interstellar debuted globally in late 2014 to massive success .
cards.readerQuestion: When and how did the film make its grand debut to global audiences?
cards.keyTakeaway: Interstellar debuted globally in late 2014 to massive success.

cards.title: Interstellar's Global Cinematic Triumph
cards.summary: Christopher Nolan's ambitious sci-fi epic captured massive audience attention and critical acclaim for its groundbreaking scientific realism and visual effects. Grossing $681 million worldwide during its initial theatrical run, the film eventually reached $774.7 million after subsequent re-releases. This massive commercial success propelled the film to rank as the 10th-highest-grossing film of 2014 globally. Audiences and critics worldwide embraced the spectacle, securing prestigious industry recognition and culminating in an Academy Award win. At the 87th Academy Awards, the film received five nominations, winning the Oscar for Best Visual Effects. Ultimately, Interstellar achieved massive global box office success and Oscar glory.
cards.readerQuestion: How did critics, global audiences, and the Academy receive the sci-fi epic?
cards.keyTakeaway: Interstellar achieved massive global box office success and Oscar glory.

cards.title: Lynda Obst's Final Cosmic Journey
cards.summary: Interstellar stands as the final film produced by Lynda Obst before her passing. To conceive this scientifically grounded space exploration epic, Obst had collaborated with physicist Kip Thorne as early as 2006. Their partnership originated that same year from a treatment the producer and theoretical physicist co-wrote together. Ultimately, the film achieved massive critical and commercial success, securing its place as a landmark science fiction masterpiece and the crowning achievement of Obst's career. It went on to gross over $774 million worldwide and won the Academy Award for Best Visual Effects. Today, Interstellar remains Obst's final, monumental cinematic legacy.
cards.readerQuestion: What is the lasting significance of the film in the career of its pioneering producer?
cards.keyTakeaway: Interstellar stands as Lynda Obst's final, monumental cinematic legacy.
